stimulus: Paleontologist Maria Reyes and her team have unearthed what they describe as the oldest known winged insect, preserved in a 312-million-year-old slab of shale in Massachusetts. The team has named the new species Volusalis paleozoica. They argue that the find pushes the evolution of flight in insects back into the Carboniferous period, which lasted from about 299 million to 359 million years ago.
stem: According to the text, how old is the insect fossil that Reyes and her team discovered?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because the text directly states that Reyes and her team unearthed an insect preserved in a 312-million-year-old slab of shale.

Choice A is incorrect because 299 million years ago marks the end of the Carboniferous period in the text, not the age of the fossil itself. Choice C is incorrect because 359 million years ago marks the beginning of the Carboniferous period, not the age of the fossil. Choice D is incorrect because the text gives no age of 275 million years; this falls outside the Carboniferous window the team mentions.
